title: Zammad
description: Open-source web-based help desk and customer support / ticketing system with email, chat, social and a knowledge base.
installation: Zammad is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack. All Zammad roles share ONE image (ghcr.io/zammad/zammad) and differ only by their entrypoint command. This template runs the PRIMARY web tier, command 'zammad-nginx' (HTTP on port 8080). To make Zammad fully functional you MUST also deploy these companion services from the SAME ghcr.io/zammad/zammad image with these commands, all sharing the same env vars and the zammad-storage (/opt/zammad/storage) volume: 'zammad-init' (run-once DB migrate/seed; must finish before the others come up), 'zammad-railsserver' (Rails app/API, internal :3000 that nginx proxies to), 'zammad-websocket' (websocket/ActionCable), 'zammad-scheduler' (background jobs / delayed jobs). An optional 'zammad-backup' container handles dumps. Bundled add-ons here cover the datastores: postgres (primary DB), memcached (Rails cache), and valkey (Redis-compatible, used for the websocket session store and job queue). Full-text search needs Elasticsearch, which is NOT one of the available operator add-ons and is omitted: deploy an Elasticsearch instance separately (image elasticsearch:9, single-node, xpack.security.enabled=false, vm.max_map_count=262144) and set ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLED=true / ELASTICSEARCH_HOST / ELASTICSEARCH_PORT to enable search; Zammad boots and works without it (search disabled). Rotate POSTGRES_PASS (default 'zammad') before exposing publicly. Set ZAMMAD_FQDN to your real hostname. On first load the web UI runs the getting-started wizard to create the first admin/organization; configure inbound/outbound email channels there afterwards.
